romania.title: Cristian Chivu va fi primit de papa Leon: cu ce ocazie merge antrenorul român în audiență
romania.summary: Antrenorul român de fotbal Cristian Chivu are parte azi de o onoare deosebită: alături de echipa sa, Internazionale Milano, va fi primit în audiență de papa Leon, după ce a câștigat campionatul Italiei.

romania.title: „Orwell: 2+2=5”, documentarul lui Raoul Peck despre manipularea realității, din 8 mai în cinematografe
romania.summary: Într-o epocă a post-adevărului, în care sensurile se schimbă și faptele sunt tot mai des contestate, „Orwell: 2+2=5” aduce universul lui „1984” în realitatea de azi. Prezentat la Festivalul de… Articolul „Orwell: 2+2=5”, documentarul lui Raoul Peck despre manipularea realității, din 8 mai în cinematografe apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: Festivalul Filmului European, ediția 30, la Cinema Studio la Timișoara

romania.title: MINA Cluj lansează spectacolele „Zeii Olimpului” și „În inima oceanului”
romania.summary: MINA Cluj, cel mai mare centru de artă imersivă din Europa, lansează din 9 mai două noi spectacole dedicate în special copiilor și familiilor: „Zeii Olimpului” și „În inima oceanului”.… Articolul MINA Cluj lansează spectacolele „Zeii Olimpului” și „În inima oceanului” apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.summary: Elevii își vor putea alege parcursul educațional în funcție de ceea ce îi interesează Leadership, antreprenoriat, astronomie, aplicații digitale inteligente, genetică aplicată și biotehnologii moderne, printre opționalele oferite de Little… Articolul Little London, desemnată școală-pilot de Ministerul Educației pentru noile planuri-cadru pentru liceu apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: Casa Ronald McDonald România își reafirmă misiunea de a oferi familiilor servicii esențiale în cele mai grele momente, atunci când copiii lor au nevoie de îngrijiri medicale.
romania.summary: Fundația pentru Copii Ronald McDonald anunță tranziția către o nouă identitate vizuală și un nou nume: Casa Ronald McDonald România, reafirmându-și misiunea de a oferi familiilor servicii esențiale în cele… Articolul Casa Ronald McDonald România își reafirmă misiunea de a oferi familiilor servicii esențiale în cele mai grele momente, atunci când copiii lor au nevoie de îngrijiri medicale. apare prima dată în Romania Pozitiva .

romania.title: 😂 Chinez pe nume Fu Sei, expulzat din Timişoara pentru că suna cam olteneşte
romania.summary: Nu doar America e din ce în ce mai ostilă cu imigranţii! În premieră, un turist chinez de 42 de ani pe nume Fu Sei a fost deportat din Timişoara după ce autorităţilor locale li s-a părut că numele lui are „o sonoritate cam oltenească”. „Ştiţi vorba aia, better safe than sorry”, şi-a explicat şeful […] The post Chinez pe nume Fu Sei, expulzat din Timişoara pentru că suna cam olteneşte appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: 😂 Adio, selfie-uri! Tot mai mulţi fermieri stropesc rapiţa cu soluţie împotriva hipsterilor
romania.summary: Veşti bune pentru agricultură, dar proaste pentru producţia de like-uri şi inimioare! Sătui să îşi găsească rapiţa călcată în picioare în fiecare weekend, tot mai mulţi fermieri apelează la tratamente împotriva hipsterilor. „Nu e chiar hipstericid, că ăla e interzis de convenţiile internaţionale. Doar îi alungă”, spune Gelu, un ţăran din Ialomiţa care îşi stropeşte […] The post Adio, selfie-uri! Tot mai mulţi fermieri stropesc rapiţa cu soluţie împotriva hipsterilor appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: 😂 În sfârșit! Românii vor fi obligați să obțină permis de port boxă portabilă, exact ca la arme
romania.summary: Gata cu coalarii care își etalează gusturile muzicale precare în spațiul public folosindu-se de o boxă portabilă! Începând cu această lună, deținerea unei boxe portabile nu va mai fi la îndemâna oricui: cei care vor una vor trebui să obțină un permis de port boxă portabilă. „Boxele portabile vor avea același regim ca armele, cum […] The post În sfârșit! Românii vor fi obligați să obțină permis de port boxă portabilă, exact ca la arme appeared first on Times New Roman .

romania.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
romania.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

romania.summary: Un drum construit prin educație Simona este elevă în clasa a XI-a, la specializarea Tehnician în industria alimentară, la un... Articolul Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.title: Psiholog
romania.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

romania.title: „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase.
romania.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

romania.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
romania.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

romania.title: Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești”
romania.summary: United Way România lucrează acolo unde nevoia este cea mai mare, acolo unde sprijinul nu este doar util, ci vital. Fără acest ajutor, mulți copii ar rămâne în urmă, riscând să se piardă în dificultățile vieții. De aceea, apreciem sprijinul pe termen lung pe care Raiffeisen Bank îl oferă programului „Învață să reușești”. Printre copiii […] Articolul Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești” apare prima dată în United Way Romania .

medical.title: Adult ADHD care may need rethink as symptom severity, not medication alone, drives deficits
medical.summary: ADHD is usually associated with being careless, impulsive and having difficulty concentrating. New research focused on ADHD in adults indicates a greater number of cognitive and motor symptoms, and that the effects of medication vary depending on the severity of symptoms. The results could pave the way for more personalized care and better day-to-day support. The research is published in the International Journal of Neuropsychopharmacology.

medical.title: States eye aid to prop up distressed hospitals amid federal Medicaid cuts
medical.summary: At Martin Luther King, Jr. Community Hospital, just outside Los Angeles, patients on gurneys line the hallways of the emergency department waiting for care, and overflow mental health patients are consigned to outdoor tents.

medical.title: Cash prescription program associated with significant reductions in infant maltreatment investigations
medical.summary: A study published in JAMA Pediatrics provides rigorous, population-level evidence that Michigan State University's Rx Kids program, the nation's first community-wide prenatal and infant cash prescription program, is associated with substantial reductions in child maltreatment investigations among infants.

medical.title: Cancer cells are better able to resist treatments when they have an abnormal number of chromosomes
medical.summary: A new study led by NYU Langone Health researchers has found that cancer cells are better able to resist treatments when they have an abnormal number of chromosomes, the DNA strands wound up in bundles that control which genetic instructions are followed in each cell type. The findings are published in the journal Molecular Cell.

medical.title: Postpartum diabetes care falls short as many women don't receive essential A1C testing
medical.summary: Few women with postpartum-onset diabetes meet recommended A1C monitoring guidelines, highlighting a need to improve routine diabetes care—particularly among Black women—according to new research at Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health. The study is published in BMJ Open Diabetes Research & Care.

medical.title: New implant links insulin-producing cells to blood vessels, aiming to treat Type 1 diabetes
medical.summary: Researchers at McGill University and the Research Institute of the McGill University Health Center (RI-MUHC) have developed a novel device to transplant insulin-producing cells that integrates directly with existing blood vessels in the body. The technology, which showed promising results in preclinical trials, aims to overcome key challenges of emerging long-term cell-based treatments for Type 1 diabetes. As well as serving as an artificial pancreas, it potentially could be used to replace or support the function of other organs.

medical.title: New study challenges the inevitability of cognitive decline and proves that brain gain is possible at any age
medical.summary: A study recently published in Scientific Reports reveals that cognitive decline is not an inevitable part of aging. Researchers from the Center for BrainHealth at The University of Texas at Dallas have demonstrated that adults across the entire lifespan, from ages 19 to 94, can measurably improve their brain performance through continual and targeted brain-healthy practices.

medical.title: Nerve-sprouting therapy boosts coordinated walking across spinal cord injury severities in mice
medical.summary: Spinal cord injuries typically result in permanent paralysis and loss of sensation, posing significant challenges for those affected and the health care system. In most cases, spinal cord injuries are contusion injuries, in which some of the fibers are damaged by compression while others remain intact. Currently available treatment approaches can only improve functional recovery to a limited extent.

medical.title: National study examines genetic testing to inform follow-up care for cancer survivors
medical.summary: Hundreds of thousands of people diagnosed with cancer are still alive today but were never genetically tested, either because testing was not available or was not routinely offered at the time of their diagnosis. These patients are just as likely as those diagnosed today to carry a germline pathogenic variant (gPV)—a heritable genetic alteration—that increases cancer risk.

science.title: Myanmar says giant 11,000-carat ruby found in Mogok could rank among most valuable
science.summary: A huge 11,000-carat ruby has been discovered in Myanmar, state media reported Friday, one of the largest ever found in the country renowned for its precious gemstones.

science.title: Plants evolved distinct functions for two forms of a fundamental signaling molecule, study shows
science.summary: The molecule cAMP, which plays essential roles in mammalian cells, is less well understood in plants. In a new Science Advances paper, researchers from the Institute of Science and Technology Austria (ISTA) and international collaborators demonstrate that plants use two forms of cAMP in parallel to regulate normal cellular processes and respond to stress, while maintaining crosstalk between them.

science.title: The moon's largest impact crater scattered something priceless—and Artemis may be heading straight into it
science.summary: A new study, published in Science Advances, has refined some important details about the moon's largest and oldest impact crater, which stretches more than 1,200 miles (2,000 km) on the far side of the moon. The new details can help guide some of the planning for NASA's upcoming Artemis mission to the moon, which is planned for 2028.

science.title: Metamorphosis in newts proves costly, with one sex paying a heavier price
science.summary: Metamorphosis, that profound transformation enabling certain animals to shift between habitats such as from an aquatic to a terrestrial environment, is generally viewed in terms of its benefits. A team of researchers from the University of Liège has now demonstrated that it also entails a direct and immediate cost for the individuals undergoing it, a cost that varies by sex and could influence long-term evolutionary trade-offs. The study is published in the journal BMC Biology.

science.title: Spaceflight leaves astronauts' joints unchanged after 18 days on ISS, early data suggest
science.summary: Researchers at National Jewish Health have published new findings demonstrating that short-duration spaceflight may not significantly impact lower extremity joint structures, while also identifying a promising, noninvasive tool to monitor astronaut musculoskeletal health on future long-duration missions.

science.title: From flying discs to glowing orbs, these newly opened Pentagon files point somewhere stranger than expected
science.summary: The Pentagon on Friday released a first batch of secret files documenting reported sightings of unidentified flying objects—some dating back to the 1940s—fanning speculation over whether alien life exists.

science.title: Spiral galaxy's brilliant heart shines bright in a new picture from NASA's Webb telescope
science.summary: A spiral galaxy's brilliant heart outshines everything within sight in a new picture from NASA's Webb Space Telescope.

science.title: Vast atmospheric waves on Venus are caused by largest known 'hydraulic jump'
science.summary: The mysterious origin of an impressive cloud disturbance on Venus has now been revealed by a team including the University of Tokyo. Researchers used numerical models to show that an enormous 6,000-kilometer-wide atmospheric wave front, which circumnavigates the planet for days at a time, is caused by a large "hydraulic jump." This is when a fluid abruptly slows down, changing from shallow and fast to deep and slow.

science.title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
science.summary: For decades, NASA has advanced on-board spacecraft computer processors that coordinate and execute the functions needed to support mission success. Space computing originated in the 1960s with the Apollo Guidance Computers, which were pivotal for guidance, navigation, and control computations during NASA’s first Moon missions. For decades, radiation-hardened processors have been the backbone of the […]

science.title: Scientists split gentoo penguins into four species, one totally new to science
science.summary: The four-foot-tall Emperor penguin of Antarctica may be the most iconic member of this unique family of birds, but 17 other species of penguins populate the Southern Hemisphere, many of them confined to isolated islands that make them hard to study.

science.title: Old bottles and battery acid can drive production of valuable industrial chemicals
science.summary: Battery acid from old cars, with a little help from a catalyst, can give plastic waste a new purpose, using it to drive the production of useful chemicals, powered by sunlight alone. A recent study by researchers at the University of Cambridge found a way to turn everyday plastics such as PET from water bottles, nylon, and polyurethane into useful chemical feedstocks.

science.title: Why infected stink bugs lift their wings: Hidden parasite escape caught on camera
science.summary: Male strepsipterans develop inside a host insect during their larval stage and, upon reaching the adult stage, emerge from the host body to begin a free-living phase. In a new study, researchers at University of Tsukuba directly observed the emergence behavior of male strepsipterans parasitizing stink bugs, where emergence sites are concealed beneath the host's wings, and found that, during emergence, the host exhibits a characteristic wing-raising behavior.

environment.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
environment.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

environment.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
environment.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

environment.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
environment.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

environment.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
environment.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

environment.title: Lake Coatepeque
environment.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

environment.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
environment.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

environment.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
environment.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

environment.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
environment.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

mix_items.title: Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București!
mix_items.summary: Cine suntem noi și de cine avem nevoie? Suntem echipa de la Telefonul Vârstnicului, serviciu social licențiat, singura linie telefonică gratuită și confidențială, accesibilă la nivel național, dedicată persoanelor vârstnice, ce oferă consiliere socială și alinarea singurătății. În fiecare an suntem sunați de peste 5,000... Post-ul Angajăm Asistent Social pentru Telefonul Vârstnicului la București! apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas
mix_items.summary: Un drum construit prin educație Simona este elevă în clasa a XI-a, la specializarea Tehnician în industria alimentară, la un... Articolul Simona, eleva care descoperă viitorul industriei alimentare pas cu pas apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale
mix_items.summary: București, aprilie 2026 – Într-o lume în care cererea de hrană crește accelerat, iar presiunea asupra resurselor naturale este tot... Articolul Agricultura viitorului: World Vision România sprijină elevii din liceele agricole să devină fermierii erei digitale apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: Psiholog
mix_items.summary: Locație: București | Nivel carieră: Experiență de minimum 1 an Candidatul Ideal: Ești tu vocea de care au nevoie? Un copil are nevoie de iubire, siguranță și încredere pentru a... The post Psiholog appeared first on SOS Satele Copiilor România .

mix_items.title: Myanmar says giant 11,000-carat ruby found in Mogok could rank among most valuable
mix_items.summary: A huge 11,000-carat ruby has been discovered in Myanmar, state media reported Friday, one of the largest ever found in the country renowned for its precious gemstones.

mix_items.title: „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase.
mix_items.summary: Într-o casă modestă din județul Vaslui, unde mirosul de lemne arse se amestecă cu zgomotul pașilor mici de copii și... Articolul „Școala e drumul vostru”. Învățăturile unei mame cu 10 clase. apare prima dată în World Vision România .

mix_items.title: De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță
mix_items.summary: În timp ce pentru mulți români Paștele este sărbătoarea revederii și a meselor în familie, pentru mulți vârstnici singuri aceste zile sunt printre cele mai grele din an. Singurătatea doare, lipsurile și sentimentul de abandon se simt mai apăsat tocmai atunci când toți ceilalți par... Post-ul De Paște, 900 de vârstnici singuri primesc prezență umană, ascultare și speranță apare prima dată în Fundația Regală Margareta a României .

mix_items.title: Plants evolved distinct functions for two forms of a fundamental signaling molecule, study shows
mix_items.summary: The molecule cAMP, which plays essential roles in mammalian cells, is less well understood in plants. In a new Science Advances paper, researchers from the Institute of Science and Technology Austria (ISTA) and international collaborators demonstrate that plants use two forms of cAMP in parallel to regulate normal cellular processes and respond to stress, while maintaining crosstalk between them.

mix_items.title: Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești”
mix_items.summary: United Way România lucrează acolo unde nevoia este cea mai mare, acolo unde sprijinul nu este doar util, ci vital. Fără acest ajutor, mulți copii ar rămâne în urmă, riscând să se piardă în dificultățile vieții. De aceea, apreciem sprijinul pe termen lung pe care Raiffeisen Bank îl oferă programului „Învață să reușești”. Printre copiii […] Articolul Sprijinul Raiffeisen Bank schimbă vieți. Povestea lui Victor din programul „Învață să reușești” apare prima dată în United Way Romania .

mix_items.title: The moon's largest impact crater scattered something priceless—and Artemis may be heading straight into it
mix_items.summary: A new study, published in Science Advances, has refined some important details about the moon's largest and oldest impact crater, which stretches more than 1,200 miles (2,000 km) on the far side of the moon. The new details can help guide some of the planning for NASA's upcoming Artemis mission to the moon, which is planned for 2028.

mix_items.title: Metamorphosis in newts proves costly, with one sex paying a heavier price
mix_items.summary: Metamorphosis, that profound transformation enabling certain animals to shift between habitats such as from an aquatic to a terrestrial environment, is generally viewed in terms of its benefits. A team of researchers from the University of Liège has now demonstrated that it also entails a direct and immediate cost for the individuals undergoing it, a cost that varies by sex and could influence long-term evolutionary trade-offs. The study is published in the journal BMC Biology.

mix_items.title: Spaceflight leaves astronauts' joints unchanged after 18 days on ISS, early data suggest
mix_items.summary: Researchers at National Jewish Health have published new findings demonstrating that short-duration spaceflight may not significantly impact lower extremity joint structures, while also identifying a promising, noninvasive tool to monitor astronaut musculoskeletal health on future long-duration missions.

mix_items.title: From flying discs to glowing orbs, these newly opened Pentagon files point somewhere stranger than expected
mix_items.summary: The Pentagon on Friday released a first batch of secret files documenting reported sightings of unidentified flying objects—some dating back to the 1940s—fanning speculation over whether alien life exists.

mix_items.title: Spiral galaxy's brilliant heart shines bright in a new picture from NASA's Webb telescope
mix_items.summary: A spiral galaxy's brilliant heart outshines everything within sight in a new picture from NASA's Webb Space Telescope.

mix_items.title: Vast atmospheric waves on Venus are caused by largest known 'hydraulic jump'
mix_items.summary: The mysterious origin of an impressive cloud disturbance on Venus has now been revealed by a team including the University of Tokyo. Researchers used numerical models to show that an enormous 6,000-kilometer-wide atmospheric wave front, which circumnavigates the planet for days at a time, is caused by a large "hydraulic jump." This is when a fluid abruptly slows down, changing from shallow and fast to deep and slow.

mix_items.title: NASA, Industry Advance High Performance Spaceflight Computing
mix_items.summary: For decades, NASA has advanced on-board spacecraft computer processors that coordinate and execute the functions needed to support mission success. Space computing originated in the 1960s with the Apollo Guidance Computers, which were pivotal for guidance, navigation, and control computations during NASA’s first Moon missions. For decades, radiation-hardened processors have been the backbone of the […]

mix_items.title: Scientists split gentoo penguins into four species, one totally new to science
mix_items.summary: The four-foot-tall Emperor penguin of Antarctica may be the most iconic member of this unique family of birds, but 17 other species of penguins populate the Southern Hemisphere, many of them confined to isolated islands that make them hard to study.

mix_items.title: Old bottles and battery acid can drive production of valuable industrial chemicals
mix_items.summary: Battery acid from old cars, with a little help from a catalyst, can give plastic waste a new purpose, using it to drive the production of useful chemicals, powered by sunlight alone. A recent study by researchers at the University of Cambridge found a way to turn everyday plastics such as PET from water bottles, nylon, and polyurethane into useful chemical feedstocks.

mix_items.title: Why infected stink bugs lift their wings: Hidden parasite escape caught on camera
mix_items.summary: Male strepsipterans develop inside a host insect during their larval stage and, upon reaching the adult stage, emerge from the host body to begin a free-living phase. In a new study, researchers at University of Tsukuba directly observed the emergence behavior of male strepsipterans parasitizing stink bugs, where emergence sites are concealed beneath the host's wings, and found that, during emergence, the host exhibits a characteristic wing-raising behavior.

mix_items.title: Adult ADHD care may need rethink as symptom severity, not medication alone, drives deficits
mix_items.summary: ADHD is usually associated with being careless, impulsive and having difficulty concentrating. New research focused on ADHD in adults indicates a greater number of cognitive and motor symptoms, and that the effects of medication vary depending on the severity of symptoms. The results could pave the way for more personalized care and better day-to-day support. The research is published in the International Journal of Neuropsychopharmacology.

mix_items.title: States eye aid to prop up distressed hospitals amid federal Medicaid cuts
mix_items.summary: At Martin Luther King, Jr. Community Hospital, just outside Los Angeles, patients on gurneys line the hallways of the emergency department waiting for care, and overflow mental health patients are consigned to outdoor tents.

mix_items.title: Cash prescription program associated with significant reductions in infant maltreatment investigations
mix_items.summary: A study published in JAMA Pediatrics provides rigorous, population-level evidence that Michigan State University's Rx Kids program, the nation's first community-wide prenatal and infant cash prescription program, is associated with substantial reductions in child maltreatment investigations among infants.

mix_items.title: Cancer cells are better able to resist treatments when they have an abnormal number of chromosomes
mix_items.summary: A new study led by NYU Langone Health researchers has found that cancer cells are better able to resist treatments when they have an abnormal number of chromosomes, the DNA strands wound up in bundles that control which genetic instructions are followed in each cell type. The findings are published in the journal Molecular Cell.

mix_items.title: Postpartum diabetes care falls short as many women don't receive essential A1C testing
mix_items.summary: Few women with postpartum-onset diabetes meet recommended A1C monitoring guidelines, highlighting a need to improve routine diabetes care—particularly among Black women—according to new research at Columbia University Mailman School of Public Health. The study is published in BMJ Open Diabetes Research & Care.

mix_items.title: New implant links insulin-producing cells to blood vessels, aiming to treat Type 1 diabetes
mix_items.summary: Researchers at McGill University and the Research Institute of the McGill University Health Center (RI-MUHC) have developed a novel device to transplant insulin-producing cells that integrates directly with existing blood vessels in the body. The technology, which showed promising results in preclinical trials, aims to overcome key challenges of emerging long-term cell-based treatments for Type 1 diabetes. As well as serving as an artificial pancreas, it potentially could be used to replace or support the function of other organs.

mix_items.title: New study challenges the inevitability of cognitive decline and proves that brain gain is possible at any age
mix_items.summary: A study recently published in Scientific Reports reveals that cognitive decline is not an inevitable part of aging. Researchers from the Center for BrainHealth at The University of Texas at Dallas have demonstrated that adults across the entire lifespan, from ages 19 to 94, can measurably improve their brain performance through continual and targeted brain-healthy practices.

mix_items.title: Nerve-sprouting therapy boosts coordinated walking across spinal cord injury severities in mice
mix_items.summary: Spinal cord injuries typically result in permanent paralysis and loss of sensation, posing significant challenges for those affected and the health care system. In most cases, spinal cord injuries are contusion injuries, in which some of the fibers are damaged by compression while others remain intact. Currently available treatment approaches can only improve functional recovery to a limited extent.

mix_items.title: National study examines genetic testing to inform follow-up care for cancer survivors
mix_items.summary: Hundreds of thousands of people diagnosed with cancer are still alive today but were never genetically tested, either because testing was not available or was not routinely offered at the time of their diagnosis. These patients are just as likely as those diagnosed today to carry a germline pathogenic variant (gPV)—a heritable genetic alteration—that increases cancer risk.

mix_items.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
mix_items.summary: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dinosaurs. The fossil of Alnashetri cerropoliciensis reveals that these animals became tiny before developing their later specialized features, such as stubby arms and ant-eating adaptations. Weighing under two pounds, the dinosaur is one of the smallest known from South America.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
mix_items.summary: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell division. By studying related yeast species, researchers found centromeres that appear to represent evolutionary halfway points. These structures seem to have formed from retrotransposons—mobile “jumping genes” in the genome. The discovery shows how DNA once considered genomic junk can be transformed into essential chromosome machinery.

mix_items.title: Light-guided evolution creates proteins that can switch, sense, and compute
mix_items.summary: Researchers have created a method called optovolution that uses light to guide the evolution of proteins with dynamic behaviors. By engineering yeast cells so their survival depended on proteins switching states at the right time, scientists could rapidly select the best-performing variants. The technique produced new light-sensitive proteins that respond to different colors and improved optogenetic systems. It even evolved a protein that behaves like a tiny logic gate, activating genes only when two signals are present.

mix_items.title: Scientists discover hidden species among Borneo’s “fanged frogs”
mix_items.summary: DNA is revealing that many animals once thought to be a single species may actually be several hidden ones. But research on Bornean fanged frogs shows the line between species can be blurry—an important challenge when deciding what wildlife needs protection most.

mix_items.title: Lake Coatepeque
mix_items.summary: Set amid El Salvador’s modern, active volcanic landscape, tranquil blue waters fill a caldera formed by ancient eruptions. The post Lake Coatepeque appeared first on NASA Science .

mix_items.title: Scientists stunned to find signs of ancient life in a place no one expected
mix_items.summary: While exploring ancient seabeds in Morocco, scientists discovered strange wrinkle-like textures in deep-water sediments that shouldn’t have been there. These structures are usually made by sunlight-loving microbial mats in shallow waters. But the rocks formed far below the reach of light, suggesting a different explanation. Evidence points to chemosynthetic microbes—organisms powered by chemical reactions—creating the mats in the dark depths of an ancient ocean.

mix_items.title: Scientists finally reveal why mint feels cold
mix_items.summary: Scientists have revealed how the body’s microscopic cold sensor, TRPM8, detects both chilly temperatures and the cooling effect of menthol. The discovery finally shows how the sensation of “cool” works at the molecular level—and could inspire new treatments for pain and eye disorders.

mix_items.title: Bird droppings helped build one of ancient Peru’s most powerful kingdoms
mix_items.summary: New research suggests seabird guano helped transform the Chincha Kingdom into one of the most prosperous societies in ancient Peru. Chemical clues in centuries-old maize show farmers fertilized their crops with guano gathered from nearby islands, dramatically boosting yields in the desert landscape. The resulting agricultural surplus fueled trade, population growth, and regional influence.

joke_ro.text: Am inchis 20 de tab-uri si m-am simtit vindecat.

satire_ro.title: Compromis. WizzAir nu scumpește biletele de avion, dar trebuie să ții în brațe alt pasager
